response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a capable full-stack and distributed-web engineer with genuine breadth across languages and systems, but she does not meet the core requirements of this role. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience as a hard minimum; her C++ exposure totals roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not appear in her skills section at all. She also lacks the cloud compute platform depth the role requires — her cloud exposure is incidental (AWS CDK for a pricing team, Kubernetes for a personal open-source project) rather than the production-level cloud architecture and infrastructure ownership expected of a technical lead in this domain. Her background is firmly rooted in web and product engineering, not systems-level compute, storage, or hardware architecture.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O), showing systems-minded engineering beyond typical web work
Staff-level and founding roles, community workshops, conference talks, and open-source project leadership indicate genuine technical leadership and mentoring capability
Consistent application of engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) across multiple organizations and tech stacks
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments, one thesis project) against the required 8 years — the single hardest disqualifier for this role
No evidence of owning or architecting a cloud compute platform; cloud touchpoints are shallow and secondary to product delivery goals
Core competency is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, DLang) — not systems programming, hardware architecture, or the compute/storage infrastructure this role centers on

key_factors.factor: Technical leadership and mentoring track record
